domains.name: Strategic Account Planning
domains.description: Structured planning and orchestration for strategic and key accounts, relationship mapping (org charts, influence and sentiment tracking), white-space mapping (which products bought vs available), joint account plans, mutual close/onboarding plans, QBR automation, and key-account metrics. Distinct from CRM (transactional opportunity record), MA (programmatic engagement), and CPQ (quoting).

domains.name: Accounting Practice Management
domains.description: CPA / tax-preparer practice operating platform: client and engagement management, workflow templates per service line (1040, 1120, 1065, audit, bookkeeping, advisory), recurring-task generation against tax-calendar deadlines, time and billing, engagement-letter and proposal management, secure client portal for document exchange, and capacity-vs-due-date analytics. Buyer is the CPA firm or tax-prep practice, solo to mid-size. IRS Pub 4557 and GLBA regulate the preparer; SOC 2 is commercially expected but voluntary.

modules.name: Client Portal and Time and Billing
modules.description: Secure client portal for document upload and e-signature with client-task-list tracking, plus timekeeper time-entry per engagement, value-billing and hourly options, WIP and AR reporting, and fee-quote-to-invoice workflow. The client-facing surface and the monetization surface of the practice.

modules.name: Engagement and Service-Line Workflow
modules.description: Per-engagement workflow templates and task assignment across service lines, sign-off checkpoints and partner-review routing, tax-calendar-driven recurring deadlines, and engagement-letter and proposal management with recurring annual re-engagement. Owns the engagement record as the workflow envelope that scopes the work.

modules.name: Tax Return Preparation and Document Retention
modules.description: Tax-return preparation and filing lifecycle (in-prep, review, ready-for-signature, filed, accepted, rejected) and client-folder document storage with preparer retention rules, prior-year carryforward, and classification by year and form type. Publishes return-state transitions to downstream compliance and billing consumers.

domains.name: Agency Management Software
domains.description: Vertical operations platform for advertising, marketing, creative, PR, and media agencies. Combines agency-specific job/traffic management, time tracking with client rate cards, retainer accounting with burn-down, media buying (plans + insertion orders + commission), creative review/proofing, multi-rate billing (cost-plus, retainer, T&M, fixed fee), and per-job profitability. Distinct from PSA (services/consulting firms — no media/retainer/commission semantics), from WORK-MGMT (horizontal, no client-billing or media-buying), and from DAM (asset library only).

modules.name: Creative Operations and Proofing
modules.description: Creative production lifecycle from the initiating brief through deliverable authoring and multi-stakeholder proofing: brief origination and client approval, deliverable version history, annotated review and approval routing, and final-version registration to the asset library.

modules.name: Job and Traffic Management
modules.description: System-of-record for the agency book of business: client jobs with traffic state and deliverable rollup, time capture against client rate cards, retainer agreements with live burn-down, pre-engagement estimating with change orders, cost-plus / markup billing logic, and per-job and per-client profitability rollups.

domains.name: AIOps
domains.description: Machine-learning-driven event correlation, anomaly detection, and noise reduction for IT operations.

modules.name: Event Correlation
modules.description: The real-time signal-reduction half of AIOps. Ingests high-volume events, alerts, metrics, and traces from the monitoring estate, detects anomalies against learned baselines, correlates and deduplicates related signals into single incidents, and applies suppression rules (including planned-change windows) to cut alert noise. Masters event_correlations, anomaly_detections, and alert_suppression_rules; consumes monitoring_events, monitoring_alerts, distributed_traces, and configuration_items owned by OBS / RMM / CMDB. Used standalone by ops teams that only need noise reduction, or alongside Predictive Intelligence for the full closed loop.

processes.name: Monitor and report events influencing factors

modules.name: Predictive Intelligence
modules.description: The analytical, forward-looking half of AIOps. Forecasts incidents and capacity problems, elevates predictive signals above action thresholds, infers probable root cause for correlated incidents, and runs the ML training lifecycle (including drift detection) that keeps the detection and prediction models trustworthy. Masters predictive_signals, incident_predictions, root_cause_analyses, and ml_model_training_records; consumes data_pipelines and configuration_items owned by DATA-AI-PLAT / CMDB. Typically deployed alongside Event Correlation, which supplies the correlated incidents that root-cause and prediction reason over.

domains.name: Knowledge Graph Platform

Includes ontology authoring, inference, SPARQL/Cypher querying, virtual-graph federation, and operational-app affordances on top of the graph store. Buyer: enterprise architect / CDO / life-sciences R&D lead. Primary JTBD: build AI agents, operational apps, and complex analytical queries on top of a unified semantic model. Distinct from MDM (master-data orchestration without ontology engine), from DATA-AI-PLAT (broader scope including pipelines / lakehouse / ML), and from a plain graph database (KGP adds reasoning, ontology governance, and ontology-mapped operational-app affordances).

modules.name: Graph Query and Applications
modules.description: Queries the unified graph, federates across remote and virtual sources without ETL, and grounds AI agents and operational apps in typed, lineage-tracked facts. Runs federated graph queries with named-graph access control, exposes relational and API sources as native graph data on demand, joins symbolic queries with vector search for retrieval, and lets code-first or low-code apps read and write back to the underlying graph entities.

modules.name: Ontology Engine
modules.description: Models the business domain as a formal ontology and stores it on a purpose-built graph engine that infers facts the team never asserted by hand. Authors and version-controls ontology classes, properties, and constraint shapes; runs the reasoning tier that materializes entailed relationships; and persists everything on a native graph store with transactions, replication, and backup.
